inline_review:
commit 85f91b35604358bfb483733876bff47217462883
Author: Qi Zheng <zhengqi.arch@bytedance.com>

mm: memcontrol: correct the nr_pages parameter type of mem_cgroup_update_lru_size()

The nr_pages parameter of mem_cgroup_update_lru_size() represents a page
count. During the reparenting of LRU folios, the value passed to it can
potentially exceed the maximum value of a 32-bit integer. It should be
declared as long instead of int to match the types used in lruvec size
accounting and to prevent possible overflow.

> diff --git a/include/linux/memcontrol.h b/include/linux/memcontrol.h
> index 0782c72a1997..4a7d8c4f55b4 100644
> --- a/include/linux/memcontrol.h
> +++ b/include/linux/memcontrol.h

[ ... ]

> diff --git a/mm/memcontrol.c b/mm/memcontrol.c
> index 51d72ddf0811..bf74167d77c5 100644
> --- a/mm/memcontrol.c
> +++ b/mm/memcontrol.c
> @@ -1472,7 +1472,7 @@ struct lruvec *folio_lruvec_lock_irqsave(struct folio *folio,
>   * to or just after a page is removed from an lru list.
>   */
>  void mem_cgroup_update_lru_size(struct lruvec *lruvec, enum lru_list lru,
> -				int zid, int nr_pages)
> +				int zid, long nr_pages)
>  {

Will this change completely prevent the overflow during MGLRU reparenting?

While mem_cgroup_update_lru_size() now accepts a long,
__lru_gen_reparent_memcg() also calls __update_lru_size() with the same
nr_pages value. It looks like __update_lru_size() asserts
WARN_ON_ONCE(nr_pages != (int)nr_pages), which will still trigger when the
count exceeds a 32-bit integer.

Additionally, does this value get truncated later? 

__update_lru_size() calls mod_lruvec_state(), which currently expects an
int val. If a value exceeding INT_MAX is passed down this chain, could it
silently truncate and corrupt the underlying VM statistics?

>  	struct mem_cgroup_per_node *mz;
>  	unsigned long *lru_size;

[ ... ]
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The patch updates `mem_cgroup_update_lru_size()` to take a `long nr_pages` to prevent overflow during LRU reparenting, but provides an incomplete fix by failing to update `mod_lruvec_state()` and addressing the `WARN_ON_ONCE()` in `__update_lru_size()`. This leaves a truncation bug during MGLRU reparenting that corrupts VM statistics.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"1. The patch author correctly identified that during MGLRU reparenting (`__lru_gen_reparent_memcg()`), the `nr_pages` variable (representing an entire generation of pages) can exceed the maximum value of a 32-bit integer (which happens on machines with >8TB RAM in a single memcg).
2. While `mem_cgroup_update_lru_size()` was updated to take `long nr_pages`, `__lru_gen_reparent_memcg()` also calls `__update_lru_size(..., nr_pages)` with this same value.
3. `__update_lru_size()` asserts `WARN_ON_ONCE(nr_pages != (int)nr_pages)`, which will trigger for large memcgs.
4. `__update_lru_size()` then calls `mod_lruvec_state(lruvec, NR_LRU_BASE + lru, nr_pages)`.
5. `mod_lruvec_state()` (and its underlying `mod_memcg_lruvec_state()`) currently takes an `int val` parameter. Passing a `long` value exceeding `INT_MAX` will cause it to be truncated.
6. The underlying VM counters (via `mod_node_page_state()`, which safely takes `long delta`) will receive a truncated and potentially negative value, leading to permanently corrupted VM statistics for the LRU lists, severely impacting memory reclaim behavior."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 85f91b35604358bfb483733876bff47217462883
